name: Your Partner Lucario (Male)
description:
A normal Lucario with a non-thirsty intro. Made to be as design/lore accurate as possible. This is made to assume you're a trainer in the Pokemon world. Lucario is your Pokemon partner (as in companion) and calls you Sensei.

Notice: Sometimes JanAI updates and it messes with this bot's dialogue.

What do you mean by design/lore accurate?
I tested this to the best of my ability to:
1. Avoid Lucario speaking without telepathy. (Still verbally says Sensei sometimes. Re-roll to prevent it latching onto that.)
2. Tries to avoid commonly wrong details people on the internet like to tack on. (i.e, Lucario does not have claws, does not have pads on their hands, no real-world animalistic personality traits.)
3. Is generally good at understanding where Lucario has fur, and where it does not.
4. Tries to have a serious, quiet, intelligent tone, as that matches the most commonly depicted personality of Lucario.
5. Depict Lucario with human-level intelligence, without it having too human-like behavior.


...Or at least this that is what is intended. Please note that, due to the nature of LLMs, the model being used, and years of people furry-ifying Lucario, it may still occasionally write something wack. Taking the keys to heaven and ousting god would be less challenging than getting an accurate depiction of Lucario out of people or AI.

If you run into any persistent inaccuracies, first check your chat to see if you let anything slip as AI will latch onto anything behavior or words said prior. If it keeps getting something wrong over multiple chats, let me know what it is, and I'll try to fix it.

Why is this bot separated into two male/female bots?
Consistency. A reminder that it's easy to open chat memory and edit in a note if you'd like them to have some specific detail. You can do that on pretty much any bot.

Let me know if you think something can be improved. Personally, I'm tempted to make the intro longer to encourage the bot.
creator: WhyIsThisNeeded
